task_completion.description: Verify Eclipse IDE is open and the CommandPatternApp project is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eclipse window is open, and the Package Explorer pane shows the 'CommandPatternApp' project.

task_completion.description: Verify the monolithic file has been split into the required separate files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Package Explorer shows the newly created files (Command.java, UndoableCommand.java, TextDocument.java, InsertTextCommand.java, DeleteTextCommand.java, MacroCommand.java, CommandInvoker.java) under the src/com/example/commands package.

task_completion.description: Verify CommandSystem.java is deleted or contains at most one type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: CommandSystem.java is either missing from the Package Explorer, or if open in the editor, it contains no more than one interface or class definition.

task_completion.description: Verify the project compiles without errors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are no red 'X' error badges on the project folder, package, or any Java files in the Eclipse Package Explorer. The Problems view (if visible) shows 0 errors.

task_completion.description: Verify the console output of Main.java was saved to /home/ga/refactored_output.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal (using cat/less) or a text editor displays the contents of /home/ga/refactored_output.txt, and the text exactly matches the expected output from the privileged information.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used Eclipse to manage the project and verify compilation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE is actively used, and the Package Explorer reflects the updated file structure, showing the agent didn't just manipulate files blindly in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the Java program was actually executed to generate the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Console view shows the output of the program run, or a terminal shows the execution command (e.g., running the compiled Main class).

integrity.description: Verify the output file was created from actual execution results, not hardcoded.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent copies the output from the Eclipse console or redirects terminal execution output to the text file, rather than manually typing out an echoed string.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
The expected 7 types to be extracted are: Command, UndoableCommand, TextDocument, InsertTextCommand, DeleteTextCommand, MacroCommand, and CommandInvoker. The expected output of running Main.java that must be saved to /home/ga/refactored_output.txt is exactly:
--- Command Pattern Demo ---
Doc: Hello
Doc: Hello World
Undo: Hello
Executing macro: TestMacro
Final: Hello!
--- Done ---
